0.title: About All Things Music
0.text:
About All Things Music
What is All Things Music?
All Things Music is a community-driven website that documents a wide variety of songs across many different genres. It provides a friendly experience that allows users to easily explore and discover a diverse range of music.
Our Goal
Here at All Things Music, our goals are to:
Create a welcoming collaborative environment
Document our community’s favourite songs
Share favourites within the the community
Introduce a more diverse musical experience to our users

Why we Do What we Do
We believe that by recording favourite songs within our community, our users will discover a far wider variety of music than they otherwise would.
If you run into unfamiliar music jargon while exploring the website, visit our Music Glossary for quick definitions.
Contributing
If you would like to add to All Things Music’s collection of songs, visit our GitHub repository and follow the listed contribution guidelines.

2.title: Did You Know?
2.text:
🎵 Did You Know?
A collection of fascinating, unusual, and mind‑blowing music facts.

🎧 The First Music Streaming Service Was Invented in 1897
The Théâtrophone let people listen to live opera over telephone lines — basically Spotify before electricity was common.

🎸 The Most Expensive Instrument Ever Sold Cost $15.9 Million
The “Lady Blunt” Stradivarius violin sold for nearly $16M and is so valuable it’s rarely played.

🥁 The Longest Concert Lasted 453 Hours
A Canadian band played for 19 straight days, rotating members to keep the music going.

🎤 Humans Can Sing Notes Lower Than a Tuba
The world record for lowest vocal note is 0.189 Hz, far below what humans can normally hear.

🎼 The Oldest Known Song Is Over 3,400 Years Old
Called the Hurrian Hymn, it was discovered on clay tablets in ancient Syria.

🎹 Beethoven Composed His Ninth Symphony While Nearly Deaf
He couldn’t hear the orchestra — he felt the vibrations through the floor.

🎧 The Loudest Concert Ever Reached 136 dB
KISS once played so loud that fans reported permanent hearing damage.

🎤 “Happy Birthday” Used to Be Copyrighted
Restaurants avoided singing it to dodge licensing fees until the copyright was overturned in 2016.

🎸 The World’s Largest Playable Guitar Is 43 Feet Long
It weighs over 2,000 pounds and requires crane‑sized picks to play.

🤘

12.title: Music Glossary
12.text:
Music Glossary
A quick reference for musical terms you might see across the website.
Core Song Structure
Verse
The main section of a song where the story or lyrics progress. Verses often share the same melody but use different words.
Chorus
A repeated section of a song, usually featuring the main hook or message. The chorus is typically the most memorable part.
Bridge
A contrasting section that connects parts of a song (often verse and chorus) and adds variety before returning to a familiar section.
Hook
A catchy musical or lyrical idea that grabs the listener’s attention. Hooks often appear in the chorus but can appear anywhere.
Outro
The closing section of a song, used to wind things down or provide a final musical statement.
Rhythm & Tempo
Tempo
The speed of a song, usually measured in beats per minute (B.P.M). Higher B.P.M means a faster song.
BPM (Beats Per Minute)
A measurement of tempo that tells you how many beats occur in one minute of music.
Groove
The overall rhythmic feel of a song, created by how the drums, bass and other instruments interact.
Syncopation
A rhythmic effect created by emphasizing normally weak beats or off-beats, often making the music feel more energetic or unexpected.
Melody & Harmony
Riff
A short, memorable musical phrase—often played on guitar, bass or keys—that repeats and helps define the character of a song.
Motif
A small musical idea or pattern that recurs throughout a piece, helping to create a sense of unity.
Melody
The main tune of a song—the part you would usually sing or hum.
Harmony
Notes played or sung at the same time as the melody to create chords and a fuller sound.
Chord Progression
A series of chords played in a specific order. Progressions create the harmonic backbone of a song.
Sound & Production
Timbre
The unique “colour” or quality of a sound that helps you distinguish one instrument or voice from another, even when they play the same note.
Dynamics
The loudness or softness of music over time. Changes in dynamics can make a song feel more emotional or dramatic.
Mix
The way all the recorded parts of a song (vocals, drums, guitars etc.) are balanced and blended together in the final version.
Fade-In/Fade-Out
When a track gradually increases from silence (fade-in) or decreases into silence (fade-out) instead of starting or stopping abruptly.
Genre & Style Terms
Subgenre
A more specific style within a broader genre (for example: math rock is a subgenre of rock; bebop is a subgenre of jazz).
Crossover
Music that blends elements from two or more genres, often appealing to different audiences at the same time.
Improvisation
Creating music on the spot rather than playing something completely planned. Improvisation is especially common in genres like jazz and blues.
